The first aerial circumnavigation is completed by a team from the US Army.[23]
Alexander Fleming notices a bacteria-killing mold growing in his laboratory, discovering what later became known as penicillin.[24]
World War II: Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union agree on a division of Poland.[25]

enAnchors.text: German–Soviet Treaty of Friendship, Cooperation and Demarcation

D0928.en: World War II: The siege of Warsaw comes to an end.
World War II: The Drama uprising against the Bulgarian occupation in northern Greece begins.[26]
Ted Williams achieves a .406 batting average for the season, and becomes the last major league baseball player to bat .400 or better.[27]
World War II: Soviet Army troops liberate Klooga concentration camp in Estonia.
CBS makes the first color televisions available for sale to the general public, but the product is discontinued less than a month later.[28]
Fernando Rios, a Mexican tour guide in New Orleans, dies of injuries sustained in an incident of gay bashing.[29]
A military coup in Damascus effectively ends the United Arab Republic, the union between Egypt and Syria.[30]
Egyptian President Gamal Abdel Nasser dies of a heart attack in Cairo.[31]

enAnchors.text: Death and state funeral of Gamal Abdel Nasser

D0928.en: The ITT Building in New York City is bombed in protest at ITT's alleged involvement in the coup d'état in Chile.[32]
The Spaghetti House siege, in which nine people are taken hostage, takes place in London.[33]
The Democratic Progressive Party becomes the first opposition party in Taiwan.[34]
A Pakistan International Airlines flight crashes into a hill in Nepal, killing all 167 passengers and crew.[35]
The cruise ferry MS Estonia sinks in the Baltic Sea, killing 852 people.[36]
Bob Denard and a group of mercenaries take the islands of the Comoros in a coup.
Israeli Prime Minister Yitzhak Rabin and PLO Chairman Yasser Arafat sign the Interim Agreement on the West Bank and the Gaza Strip.
Al-Aqsa Intifada: Ariel Sharon visits Al-Aqsa Mosque known to Jews as the Temple Mount in Jerusalem.[37]
Typhoon Xangsane passes over Manila after impacting parts of Southern Luzon and Eastern Visayas, becoming the strongest to affect the Philippine capital in 11 years.[38]
Falcon 1 becomes the first privately developed liquid-fuel ground-launched vehicle to put a payload into orbit by the RatSat mission.[39]

The Singapore Grand Prix is held as Formula One's inaugural night race, with Fernando Alonso winning the event. Almost a year later it was revealed that Alonso's team-mate Nelson Piquet Jr. had been ordered to crash his car to help bring out the safety car and give Alonso the advantage and win.[40][41]
The military junta leading Guinea attacks a protest rally, killing or wounding 1,400 people.[42]
Somali and African Union forces launch a coordinated assault on the Somali port of Kismayo to take back the city from al-Shabaab militants.[43]
Sita Air Flight 601 crashes in Madhyapur Thimi, Nepal, killing all 19 passengers and crew.[44]
The 2014 Hong Kong protests begin in response to restrictive political reforms imposed by the NPC in Beijing.[45]
The 2016 South Australian blackout occurs, lasting up to three days in some areas.[46]
The 7.5 Mw 2018 Sulawesi earthquake, which triggered a large tsunami, leaves 4,340 dead and 10,679 injured.[47]

Hurricane Ian makes landfall in Cayo Costa State Park, Florida as a category four hurricane, killing 169 and doing $113 billion in damage, becoming Florida's costliest hurricane and the deadliest in 89 years.[48]

The 2023 Rotterdam shootings occurred, during which two people were killed in a shooting and arson incident at a residence in Delfshaven, Rotterdam. Additionally, one person lost their life in a classroom at the Erasmus University Medical Center.[49]

D0929.en: Danes capture Canterbury after a siege, taking Ælfheah, archbishop of Canterbury, as a prisoner.[2]
Frederick II, Holy Roman Emperor, is excommunicated by Pope Gregory IX for his failure to participate in the Crusades during the Investiture Controversy.[3]
The Treaty of Montgomery recognises Llywelyn ap Gruffudd as Prince of Wales, but only as a vassal of King Henry III.[4]
During the Hundred Years' War, Anglo-Breton forces defeat the Franco-Breton army in Brittany, ending the War of the Breton Succession.[5]
During the French War of Religion, Protestant coup officials in Nîmes massacre Catholic priests in an event now known as the Michelade.[6]
Tegucigalpa, capital city of Honduras, is claimed by the Spaniards.[7]
The Cossacks of the Tsardom of Russia kill about 800 people overnight in Hailuoto during the Great Wrath.[8]
An earthquake strikes Antigua Guatemala, destroying much of the city's architecture.[9]
J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Herr Gott, dich loben alle wir, BWV 130 for the feast of archangel Michael, based on Paul Eber's hymn in twelve stanzas.[10]

enAnchors.text: Herr Gott, dich loben alle wir, BWV 130

D0929.en: The United States Department of War first establishes a regular army with a strength of several hundred men.[11]
The Metropolitan Police of London, later also known as the Met, is founded.[12]
The Battle of Pákozd is a stalemate between Hungarian and Croatian forces and is the first battle of the Hungarian Revolution.[13]
The papal bull Universalis Ecclesiae restores the Roman Catholic hierarchy in England and Wales.[14]
The Philippine port of Iloilo is opened to world trade by the Spanish administration.[15][16]
The Battle of Chaffin's Farm is fought in the American Civil War.[17]
The Treaty of Lisbon defines the boundaries between Spain and Portugal and abolishes the Couto Misto microstate.[18]
The first practical public electric tramway in the world is opened in Blackpool, England.[19]
The cornerstone is laid at the Cathedral Church of Saint Peter and Saint Paul (better known as Washington National Cathedral) in Washington, D.C.[20]
Italy declares war on the Ottoman Empire, In this war , Italy gains Libya which ends Ottoman rule in North Africa .[21]
World War I:  Bulgaria signs the Armistice of Salonica ending its participation.[22]
World War I:  The Hindenburg Line is broken by an Allied attack.[23]
World War I:  Germany's Supreme Army Command tells Kaiser Wilhelm II and Imperial Chancellor Georg Michaelis to open negotiations for an armistice to end the war.[24]
Ukrainian War of Independence: The Ukrainian Soviet Socialist Republic agree to a truce with the Makhnovshchina.[25]
The Mandate for Palestine takes effect, creating Mandatory Palestine.[26]
The Mandate for Syria and Lebanon takes effect.[27]
The First American Track and Field championships for women are held.[28]

enAnchors.text: 1923 USA Outdoor Track and Field Championships

D0929.en: Last day of the Battle of Boquerón between Paraguay and Bolivia during the Chaco War.[29]
Two Avro Ansons collide in mid-air over New South Wales, Australia, remain locked together, then land safely.[30]
During World War II, German forces, with the aid of local Ukrainian collaborators, begin the two-day Babi Yar massacre.[31]
The convention establishing CERN (the European Organization for Nuclear Research) is signed.[32]
The Kyshtym disaster is the third-worst nuclear accident ever recorded.[33]

enAnchors.text: Nuclear and radiation accidents and incidents

D0929.en: A Lockheed L-188 Electra crashes in Buffalo, Texas, killing 34 people.[34]
Oman joins the Arab League.[35]
Japan establishes diplomatic relations with the People's Republic of China after breaking official ties with the Republic of China.[36]
WGPR becomes the first black-owned-and-operated television station in the US.[37]
Equatorial Guinean dictator Francisco Macías Nguema is executed by soldiers from Western Sahara.[38]
An Iranian Air Force Lockheed C-130 Hercules military transport aircraft crashes into a firing range near Kahrizak, Iran, killing 80 people.[39]

enAnchors.text: Islamic Republic of Iran Air Force

D0929.en: NASA launches STS-26, the first Space Shuttle mission since the Challenger disaster.[40]
Construction of the Cathedral Church of Saint Peter and Saint Paul (better known as Washington National Cathedral) is completed in Washington, D.C.[41]
The YF-22, which would later become the F-22 Raptor, flies for the first time.[42]
The Tampere Hall, the largest concert and congress center in the Nordic countries, is inaugurated in Tampere, Finland.[43]
A Haitian coup d'état occurs.[44]
Brazilian President Fernando Collor de Mello is impeached.[45]
The asteroid 4179 Toutatis passes within four lunar distances of Earth.[46]
Burt Rutan's Ansari SpaceShipOne performs a successful spaceflight, the first of two required to win the Ansari X Prize.[47]
John Roberts is confirmed as Chief Justice of the United States.[48]

enAnchors.text: Chief Justice of the United States

D0929.en: A Boeing 737 and an Embraer 600 collide in mid-air, killing 154 people and triggering a Brazilian aviation crisis.[49]
Calder Hall, the world's first commercial nuclear power station, is demolished in a controlled explosion.[50]

The stock market crashes with the Dow Jones dropping a then record 778 points after the United States House of Representatives vote on the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act fails during the beginning stages of the Great Recession.[51][52]
The 8.1 Mw  Samoa earthquake results in a tsunami that kills over 189 and injures hundreds.[53]
The special court in India convicted all 269 accused officials for atrocity on Dalits and 17 for rape in the Vachathi case.[54]
Over 42 people are killed by members of Boko Haram at the College of Agriculture in Nigeria.[55]
Eleven days after the Uri attack, the Indian Army conducts "surgical strikes" against suspected militants in Pakistani-administered Kashmir.[56]
Violence and low turnout mar the 2019 Afghan presidential election.[57]

D0930.en: The Ostrogoths under Theoderic the Great defeat the forces of Odoacer for the second time.[1]
The Turgesh drive back an Umayyad invasion of Khuttal, follow them south of the Oxus, and capture their baggage train.[2]
A magnitude 7.7 earthquake strikes the Caucasus mountains in the Seljuk Empire, causing mass destruction and killing up to 300,000 people.[3]
Battle of Morlaix is fought in the Hundred Years' War.[4]
Henry IV is proclaimed king of England.[5]
Suleiman the Magnificent becomes sultan of the Ottoman Empire.[6]
Spanish conquistador Hernando de Soto and his forces enter Tula territory in present-day western Arkansas, encountering fierce resistance.
A coup by the military establishment of Japan's Ōuchi clan forces their lord to commit suicide, and their city is burned.[7]

The Lebanese Council of 1736 begins, a major turning point in the reform of the Maronite Church. In the following three days, the assembled Maronite and Latin clergy presided by Yusuf ibn Siman as-Simani discuss various reforms and elaborate rules and canons.[8]
War of the Austrian Succession: France and Spain defeat Sardinia at the Battle of Madonna dell'Olmo, but soon have to withdraw from Sardinia.[9]
The first performance of Mozart's opera The Magic Flute takes place two months before his death.[10]
France's National Constituent Assembly is dissolved, to be replaced the next day by the National Legislative Assembly.[11]
Georges Bizet's opera Les pêcheurs de perles, premieres in Paris.[12]
Thomas Edison's first commercial hydroelectric power plant, the Vulcan Street Plant, begins operation.[13]
Jack the Ripper kills his third and fourth victims, Elizabeth Stride and Catherine Eddowes.[14]
The Royal Galician Academy, the Galician language's biggest linguistic authority, is established in La Coruña, Spain.[15]
The McKinley National Memorial, the final resting place of assassinated U.S. President William McKinley and his family, is dedicated in Canton, Ohio.
The Cunard Line's RMS Mauretania makes a record-breaking westbound crossing of the Atlantic, that will not be bettered for 20 years.
World War I: Radoje Ljutovac becomes the first soldier in history to shoot down an enemy aircraft with ground-to-air fire.
Ukrainian War of Independence: Insurgent forces led by Nestor Makhno defeats the Central Powers at the battle of Dibrivka.[16]
The Hoover Dam, astride the border between the U.S. states of Arizona and Nevada, is dedicated.

American journalists Herbert R. Ekins, reporter for the New York World-Telegram, Dorothy Kilgallen of the New York Journal and Leo Kieran of The New York Times start the race to travel around the world[17] on commercial airline flights. The race takes 18 ½ days.
Britain, France, Germany and Italy sign the Munich Agreement, whereby Germany annexes the Sudetenland region of Czechoslovakia.
The League of Nations unanimously outlaws "intentional bombings of civilian populations".
World War II: General Władysław Sikorski becomes prime minister of the Polish government-in-exile.
NBC broadcasts the first televised American football game.
World War II: The Babi Yar massacre comes to an end.
The United States Merchant Marine Academy is dedicated by President Roosevelt.
World War II:  the Germans commence a counter offensive to retake the Nijmegen salient, this having been captured by the allies during Operation Market Garden.
The Bourne End rail crash, in Hertfordshire, England, kills 43.

The 1947 World Series begins. It is the first to be televised, to include an African-American player, to exceed $2 million in receipts, to see a pinch-hit home run, and to have six umpires on the field.
Pakistan joins the United Nations.
The Berlin Airlift ends.
The U.S. Navy submarine USS Nautilus is commissioned as the world's first nuclear-powered vessel.
Bechuanaland declares its independence, and becomes the Republic of Botswana.
The Boeing 747 is rolled out and shown to the public for the first time.
Jordan makes a deal with the PFLP for the release of the remaining hostages from the Dawson's Field hijackings.
Malév Flight 240 crashes into the Mediterranean Sea while on approach to Beirut International Airport in Beirut, Lebanon, killing 60.[19]
Finnair Flight 405 is hijacked by Aarno Lamminparras in Oulu, Finland.[20]
Ethernet specifications are published by Xerox working with Intel and Digital Equipment Corporation.
The 6.2 Mw  Latur earthquake shakes Maharashtra, India with a maximum Mercalli intensity of VIII (Severe) killing 9,748 and injuring 30,000.
Space Shuttle Endeavour is launched on STS-68.[21]
The Tokaimura nuclear accident causes the deaths of two technicians in Japan's second-worst nuclear accident.
Israeli–Palestinian conflict: Twelve-year-old Muhammad al-Durrah is shot and killed on the second day of the Second Intifada.[22]
Controversial drawings of Muhammad are printed in a Danish newspaper.
The 7.6 Mw  Sumatra earthquake leaves 1,115 people dead.
Hurricane Matthew becomes a Category 5 hurricane, making it the strongest hurricane to form in the Caribbean Sea since 2007.
Two paintings with a combined value of $100 million are recovered after having been stolen from the Van Gogh Museum in 2002.
